title: [Seq] Add a more general-purpose [RegOp]
body:
The RegOp serves to be the everything-but-the-kitchen sink version, as per [this discourse thread](https://llvm.discourse.group/t/rfc-extending-seq-combreg-with-enables-resets-and-clears/3793/4).

```mlir
// with a negedge reset
seq.reg %d, posedge %clk, enable %enable, syncreset negedge %reset, %resetValue : !hw.struct<foo:i32> : !hw.struct<foo:i32

// with no reset
seq.reg %d, posedge %clk, enable %enable, noreset : !hw.struct<foo:i32>
```

Some implementation details:
- `%enable` is a mandatory argument. For most part, it will just be set to vdd (or `hw.constant true`)
- `%resetValue` and `%resetTo` must either both be specified, or both not be specified.

Note that the assembly for cases when there are reset is a little funky -- namely, the type of the `$input` and `$resetValue` needs to both be specified. This is mostly because we can't get tablegen to enforce an optional operand to have a type match only when it's present. I chose to have a funkier assembly format, since it's much easier than writing a custom parser & printer. I added a verifier for the RegOp to check that this requirement is enforced.

Some minor clean up:
- I shared code with as much as possible lowering compreg => SV
- I exposed some enums used in SV td to a separate file , so that we can include it in SeqOps.td
